# Import libraries
## Data manipulation
library(tidyverse)
## Statistics
library(psych)
## Visualization
library(ggplot2)
## Correlation plots
library(corrplot)
## Normality tests
library(nortest)

nvidia <- read.csv("NVDA.csv")

# Convert date column
nvidia$Date <- as.Date(nvidia$Date)

# View structure
str(nvidia)

# First rows
head(nvidia)


# --- Data Preparation ---

## Missing Values
colSums(is.na(nvidia))

## Outlier detection
boxplot(nvidia$Close, main="Boxplot of Closing Price")
boxplot(nvidia$Volume, main="Boxplot of Trading Volume")  # eliminate outliers?


# --- Descriptive Statistics ---
describe(nvidia[,c("Open","High","Low","Close","Adj.Close","Volume")])


# --- Time Series Visualization ---
ggplot(nvidia, aes(x=Date, y=Close)) +
  geom_line(color="blue") +
  labs(title="NVIDIA Closing Price Over Time",
       x="Date",
       y="Closing Price")

# New variable
nvidia <- nvidia %>%
  mutate(Return = (Close - Open)/Open)

# --- Correlation Matrix ---
cor_matrix <- cor(nvidia[,c("Open","High","Low","Close","Adj.Close","Volume")])
cor_matrix
corrplot(cor_matrix, method="color", type="upper")

# --- Normality Test ---
shapiro.test(nvidia$Close)  # error: sample size must be between 3 and 5000
qqnorm(nvidia$Close)
qqline(nvidia$Close, col="red")

# --- Confidence Interval for Mean Closing Price ---
t.test(nvidia$Close, conf.level = 0.95)
# Interpretation: We estimate with 95% confidence that the average closing price lies between X and Y.

# --- Correlation Analysis ---
cor.test(nvidia$Open, nvidia$Close)
cor.test(nvidia$Volume, nvidia$Close)
# Interpretation: If p < 0.05 → significant relationship

# --- Simple Linear Regression (Predict Close with Open) ---
model_simple <- lm(Close ~ Open, data=nvidia)
summary(model_simple)

ggplot(nvidia, aes(Open, Close)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method="lm") +
  labs(title="Regression: Open vs Close Price")

# --- Multiple Linear Regression ---
model_multiple <- lm(Close ~ Open + High + Low + Volume, data=nvidia)
summary(model_multiple)

# --- Model Diagnosis ---
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(model_multiple)

# --- Prediction Example ---
predict(model_multiple,
        newdata=data.frame(Open=900,
                           High=910,
                           Low=890,
                           Volume=40000000))
